architecture_fit.note: Single hand-built attention head, no MLP — minimal delta from base_model: additive score w_color·(colours differ)+w_adj·edge then row-softmax, directly producing color_separation>0 and edge_respect>0.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform baseline (sep≈0) and edge-only(~pass_2) strawman are both shown; uniform clearly fails, though edge-only still reaches 0.029 vs full 0.033 so its headline 'collapse' is modest.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic circuit (no trained model, acknowledged); ablation isolates the colour term as load-bearing for edge_respect and proposes the analogous Q/K-subspace patch for a trained model.

operating_range.note: n=20→320 (~16×, ~1.2 orders); separation stays strictly positive and 1/n decay is explained, slightly short of the 2-orders target.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set weights (W_COLOR=8, W_ADJ=4) on CUDA, no training — a clean by-hand circuit reproducing the target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Three legible views — ablation bar chart, colour-sorted attention heatmap, log-scale operating-range line — each mapped to a claim; app.py is structurally correct.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' ties each chart to a rubric item, justifies axes/baselines, and explains the 1/n decay as expected rather than failure.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built single attention head that directly answers the goal: an additive colour+edge bias makes attention favour differently-coloured pairs (sep 0.033 > baseline 0) and proper edges (edge_respect 0.14). The ablation, uniform/pass_2 baselines, and ~16× operating-range sweep are well presented and clearly visualised, with an excellent rationale section. Minor weaknesses: the edge-only strawman's headline 'collapse' is overstated (0.029 vs 0.033) and operating range is ~1.2 rather than 2 orders of magnitude.
